2605.27276.title: SIA: Self Improving AI with Harness & Weight Updates
2605.27276.paper_summary: Humans are the bottleneck in building and improving AI. Both the models and the agents that wrap them are written, tuned, and corrected by people. The long-horizon goal of an AI that can figure out how to improve itself remains open. Two largely disjoint research lines attack this bottleneck. The harness-update school has a meta-agent rewrite the scaffold of a task-specific agent (its tools, prompts, retry logic, and search procedure) while the model weights are held fixed. The test-time training school uses hand-written RL pipelines to update the model's own weights on task feedback while the harness is held fixed. These two silos operate in isolation. We propose SIA, a self-improving loop in which a language-model agent (the Feedback-Agent) updates both the harness and the weights of a task-specific agent. We evaluate across three contrasting domains: Chinese legal charge classification, low-level GPU kernel optimisation, and single-cell RNA denoising. Combining both levers outperforms scaffold iteration alone on all three benchmarks. The gains are 56.6% on LawBench, 91.9% runtime reduction on GPU kernels, and 502% on denoising over the initial baseline. Harness updates make the model agentic, shaping how it searches and acts, while weight updates build the domain intuition that no prompt or scaffold can instil.
2605.27276.paper_summary_zh: 人類是建立和改進人工智慧的瓶頸。無論是模型還是包裹它們的代理，都是由人編寫、調整和修正的。能夠自我改進的人工智慧的長期目標仍然開放。兩條基本不相交的研究路線攻克這一瓶頸。鞍具更新學派讓一個元代理重寫任務特定代理的框架（其工具、提示、重試邏輯和搜索程序），同時保持模型權重不變。測試時訓練學派使用手寫的強化學習管道根據任務反饋更新模型自身的權重，同時保持鞍具不變。這兩個孤島各自獨立運作。我們提出SIA，一個自我改進的循環，其中一個語言模型代理（反饋代理）同時更新鞍具和任務特定代理的權重。我們在三個對比鮮明的領域進行評估：中國法律指控分類、低層GPU內核優化和單細胞RNA去噪。結合這兩個槓桿在所有三個基準上都超越了單獨的框架迭代。在LawBench上增益為56.6%，在GPU內核上運行時間減少91.9%，在去噪上相較於初始基準增長502%。鞍具更新使模型具備代理性，塑造其搜索和行動的方式，而權重更新則建立了無法通過任何提示或框架灌輸的領域直覺。
2605.27276.author: Prannay Hebbar et.al.
2605.27276.authors: Prannay Hebbar, Yogendra Manawat, Samuel Verboomen, Alesia Ivanova, Selvam Palanimalai, Kunal Bhatia, Vignesh Baskaran
